_notes: CHERP code-dev corpus seed. Schema gotchas, dev rules, common task patterns. Each entry becomes an instruction/response pair via Codex/brain/build_v3_corpus.py.

schema_gotchas.instruction: How do I insert into crew_tasks?
schema_gotchas.output: crew_tasks.id is BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY. NEVER send a client-provided id. Use Prefer: return=representation header so the bigint id comes back in the response. Example: POST /rest/v1/crew_tasks with header Prefer: return=representation, body without id.

schema_gotchas.instruction: Why does my insert into daily_logs fail with team_code error?
schema_gotchas.output: daily_logs has NO team_code column. Use company_id (uuid) and created_by (uuid) instead. Tracking belongs to the row id, not a team_code field.

schema_gotchas.instruction: How do I write to messages table in CHERP?
schema_gotchas.output: messages has NO team_code. Use sender_id (uuid) and channel field. Content column is body, NOT content. Example: { sender_id, channel: team_code_value, body: text }.
crew_timecards uses client-generated TEXT id (not auto-increment). Columns: user_id, user_name, hours, date. Don't use worker_name or hours_worked — those don't exist.

schema_gotchas.instruction: What roles are allowed in user_profiles.role?
schema_gotchas.output: As of phase 2 ownership migration: apprentice, journeyman, foreman, general_foreman, superintendent, admin, superuser, worker. Pre-phase-2 worker was rejected. The role check constraint enforces this list.

schema_gotchas.instruction: Why is my Supabase API call silently failing in CHERP?
schema_gotchas.output: Check netlify.toml CSP. connect-src must include the Supabase project URL or the request gets blocked silently. Also check the service worker isn't serving stale config.js.

schema_gotchas.instruction: How do I add a new screen to CHERP that needs offline support?
schema_gotchas.output: Route through window.store (js/store.js) instead of direct SB() calls. store.list/get/create/update/delete go to IndexedDB first, then sync to Supabase in background. Stamp owner_id: _s.id on creates.

dev_rules.instruction: Should I push to main on CHERP without testing?
dev_rules.output: no. main auto-deploys to cherp.live via netlify. test on dev branch first. main is production.

dev_rules.instruction: Where do I save before editing CHERP code?
dev_rules.output: stash or commit to dev branch first. emergency backup zip exists at C:/Users/Ken/Desktop/cherp emergency.zip if you nuke the repo.

dev_rules.instruction: Service worker is caching stale CHERP files — how do I fix it?
dev_rules.output: three escape hatches: ?sw=off URL param, /clear-sw.html page, admin button in app. for breaking deploys, bump the cache version constant in service-worker.js.

dev_rules.instruction: How does CHERP authenticate users without Supabase?
dev_rules.output: hardcoded fallback users in js/config.js allow PIN login when Supabase is unreachable. PIN is SHA-256 hashed at signup. don't break this fallback path.

ownership_migration.instruction: What is the ownership migration status in CHERP?
ownership_migration.output: Phases 1-6 are LIVE on main as of 2026-04-13. Store.js IndexedDB cache (phase 1), additive schema (phase 2), tasks/timeclock/safety/work/certs migrated to store.js (phases 3-5), employee_id + QR scanner (phase 6a/6b). Phases 7-10 queued. See .claude/plans/cherp-ownership-system.md.

ownership_migration.instruction: What is owner_id used for in CHERP tables?
ownership_migration.output: owner_id (uuid, nullable) on crew_tasks, crew_timecards, jsa_reports, mro_equipment, daily_logs, certifications, messages, crew_jsa, crew_mros. Set to _s.id (auth user id) on creates. RLS-scoped to owner. Replaces team_code-only ownership.

ownership_migration.instruction: What does item_shares do in CHERP?
ownership_migration.output: item_shares is the first-class sharing graph. Lets workers explicitly share their tasks/timecards/etc. with other employees by employee_id, instead of relying on team_code group membership.

common_pitfalls.instruction: Why is _s.id undefined in my CHERP code?
common_pitfalls.output: _s.id is set at login from auth.userId. Before phase 6a it wasn't being set, so owner_id stamps wrote undefined. Verify _s.id = auth.userId is set in your login path. This was a critical fix during the ownership migration.

common_pitfalls.instruction: Why does my chat-log.jsonl have weird control codes?
common_pitfalls.output: ollama run leaks ANSI CSI and OSC terminal spinner codes into stdout. Strip them: replace /\u001b\[\??[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g and /\u001b\][^\u0007]*\u0007/g. server.js does this in the chat endpoint; copy that pattern in any new ollama spawn path.

common_pitfalls.instruction: How do I query CHERP from PowerShell or curl for verification?
common_pitfalls.output: use the public anon key + REST endpoint: curl 'https://nptmzihtujgkmqougkzd.supabase.co/rest/v1/<table>?select=*' -H 'apikey: <anon_key>' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <anon_key>'. table names like user_profiles, crew_tasks, item_shares.
